meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 1)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify common and proper nouns and understand capitalization rules.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Japan" is a proper noun — it's the name of a specific country. It must be capitalized.

questions.question: "Girl" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "girl" is a common noun — it refers to any girl, not a specific one.

questions.question: Which word needs to be capitalized?
questions.explanation: "Christmas" is a proper noun (a holiday name) and must always be capitalized.

questions.question: Proper nouns must always begin with a capital letter.
questions.explanation: Yes! Proper nouns (names of specific people, places, things) always start with a capital letter.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "teacher" is a common noun — it refers to any teacher, not a specific one.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Monday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: Days of the week are always capitalized
questions.explanation: Monday is a proper noun — days of the week are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: girl/teacher/city = general nouns (common). Japan/Monday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "We go to the park on saturday," the word "saturday" is correctly written.
questions.explanation: "Saturday" is a proper noun (day of the week) and must be capitalized: Saturday.

questions.question: Which sentence is written correctly?
options: We go to the park on saturday.
We go to the park on Saturday.
we go to the park on Saturday.
We go to the Park on Saturday.
questions.explanation: "Saturday" must be capitalized (proper noun — day of the week). "park" is a common noun and stays lowercase.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Steve"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Steve is a proper noun — it's a specific person's name and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Kang Chiao is in Linkou." Both are proper nouns (capitalized).

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: classroom/teacher/monkey = general names (common). Anna/Taipei = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "July" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! July is a month name — months are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has the correct capitalization?
options: It is very hot in july.
It is very hot in July.
it is very hot in July.
It is very hot in JULY.
questions.explanation: "July" is a proper noun (month name) and must be capitalized.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Christmas"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Christmas is a proper noun — it's a specific holiday name and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "We wear red for Chinese New Year." 'Chinese New Year' is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which word in this list is NOT correctly capitalized?
questions.explanation: "december" is wrong — December is a proper noun (month) and must be capitalized.

questions.question: In the sentence "The Playground is big," the word "Playground" is correctly capitalized.
questions.explanation: "playground" is a common noun — it should NOT be capitalized unless it starts a sentence.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: beach/hospital = general words (common). December/Halloween/Sunday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: The bear's name is max. Fix the proper noun: write the corrected name.
questions.hint: A name must start with a capital letter
questions.explanation: "max" should be "Max" — names are proper nouns and always start with a capital letter.
